data.title: Ryde stock slides on offerings to raise $14M
data.description:
• Ryde Group (RYDE) shares fell nearly 7% premarket on Wednesday after the mobility app announced plans to raise around US$14 million.
  • The company entered into a securities purchase agreement with several investors for a private placement of 33.33M Class A ordinary shares and a registered direct offering of 5.55M Class A ordinary shares at an offering price of US$0.36 per class A share.
  • Ryde Group (RYDE) currently intends to use the net proceeds from the offerings for working capital and general corporate purposes. The registered direct offering and private placement are expected to close on or about December 3 and December 9, 2025, respectively.

data.title: IREN prices stock, debt offerings
data.description:
IREN (IREN) priced an offering of about 39.7 million shares at $41.12 each, raising roughly $1.63 billion.

Concurrently with the pricing, IREN entered into one or more separate, privately negotiated transactions with a limited number of holders of its outstanding 3.25% convertible senior notes due 2030 and 3.50% convertible senior notes due 2029 to repurchase around $227.7 million of the existing 2030 convertible notes and ~$316.6 million of the existing 2029 convertible notes for an aggregate repurchase price of around $1,632.4 million.

IREN also priced a private offering of $1 billion in 0.25% convertible notes due 2032 and $1 billion in 1.00% convertible notes due 2033, with an option for buyers to purchase up to an additional $150 million of each.

Proceeds from the offerings will help fund capped call transactions, buy back existing convertible debt, and support general corporate purposes. The transactions are expected to close on December 8, 2025.

IREN +1.79% premarket to $41.86

Source: Press Release

data.title: EU reaches deal to end Russian gas imports by late 2027
data.description:
The European Union has reached a deal to permanently stop Russian gas imports by November 2027 and phase out Russian oil, in an effort to end the bloc's energy dependence on Russia amid the Ukraine war. 

The deal ensures a gradual but permanent end of Russian gas imports, with LNG imports phased out by Dec. 31, 2026 and pipeline gas by Sept. 30, 2027.

EU member states may extend this deadline until Oct. 31, 2027 in case their storage levels are below the required filling levels. 

The EU remains committed to phase out all remaining oil imports from Russia by the end of 2027, and a legislative proposal to ban these imports will be tabled early next year.

EU member states must submit national diversification plans for oil and gas supplies by March 1, 2026. They must also notify the European Commission whether they have Russian gas supply contracts or national bans in place.

"By depleting Putin's war chest, we stand in solidarity with Ukraine and set our sights on new energy partnerships and opportunities for the sector," said Ursula von der Leyen, the commission's president.

Russian gas accounted for 13% of the EU's overall imports in the first half of 2025, down from 45% before Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

"All imports of Russian coal have been banned by sanctions, oil imports have shrunk from 27% at the beginning of 2022 to 2% now," the EU noted.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada raises dividend by 6.5% to CAD 1.64 a share
data.description:
• Royal Bank of Canada (RY) declares CAD 1.64/share quarterly dividend, 6.5% increase from prior dividend of CAD 1.54.
  • Forward yield 3.04%
  • Payable Feb. 24; to shareholders of record Jan. 26; ex-div Jan. 26.
  • See RY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada Non-GAAP EPS of C$3.85 beats by C$0.30, revenue of C$17.21B beats by C$350M
data.description:
• Royal Bank of Canada press release (RY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$3.85 beats by C$0.30.
  • Revenue of C$17.21B (+14.2% Y/Y) beats by C$350M.

data.title: India scraps mandate for preinstalled government cybersecurity app
data.description:
India’s telecom ministry on Wednesday reversed its directive requiring smartphone makers to preinstall a government-run cybersecurity app on new devices.

"Government has decided not to make the pre-installation mandatory for mobile manufacturers," India's communications ministry said in a press statement.

The Indian government had confidentially ordered companies, including Apple (AAPL), Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF), and Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY), to preload their phones with an app called "Sanchar Saathi" within 90 days. The app was aimed at tracking stolen phones, blocking them, and preventing them from being misused.

Earlier reports said Apple had no plans to comply with the order and would inform the government that it does not follow such mandates anywhere in the world, arguing they pose privacy and security risks to the iOS ecosystem.

data.title: Yuanbao reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Yuanbao press release (YB): Q3 total revenues in the third quarter of 2025 were RMB1,157.9 million (US$162.6 million), representing a 33.6% year-over-year increase from RMB866.8 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Number of new policies in the third quarter of 2025 was 8.0 million, representing a 41.8% year-over-year increase from 5.7 million in the same period of 2024.
  • EPADS of $0.18.

data.title: AT&T commits to ending DEI programs
data.description:
AT&T (T) has committed to ending diversity, equity, and inclusion ((DEI)) programs, a step taken as it seeks FCC approval for its $1.02 billion purchase of wireless spectrum licenses from U.S. Cellular.

In an FCC filing, the U.S. wireless carrier said, “The legal landscape governing diversity, equity, and inclusion (“DEI”) policies and programs has changed. We have closely followed the recent Executive Orders, Supreme Court rulings, and guidance issued by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission and have adjusted our employment and business practices to ensure that they comply with all applicable laws and related requirements, including ending DEI-related policies as described below, not just in name but in substance.”

“AT&T does not and will not have any roles focused on DEI.” 

The move aligns with Trump administration directives, under which the FCC requires telecom companies to end DEI programs as a condition for approving transactions.

It mirrors similar actions by other carriers: T-Mobile US (TMUS) said it was ending its DEI programs while pursuing two major transactions, and Verizon (VZ) agreed to do the same before the FCC approved its $20 billion acquisition of fiber-optic internet provider Frontier Communications (FYBR).

data.title: Ads from Nike, Superdry and Lacoste banned in UK for greenwashing
data.description:
The UK's advertising watchdog has banned advertisements from Nike (NKE), Superdry and Lacoste that made misleading environmental claims, as part of the regulator's crackdown on greenwashing.

The Advertising Standards Authority said the paid-for Google ads used the terms "sustainable clothing" or "sustainable materials" without backing the claims with evidence.

Such claims could mislead consumers looking to buy more sustainable products, the regulator said.

"We considered that without qualification, the claim 'sustainable' was ambiguous and unclear," the ASA's ruling read. "The claim was absolute and therefore a high level of substantiation in support needed to be produced."

All three brands have removed the ads in question. The ASA directed the brands to ensure that the basis of future environmental claims is made clear, as well as substantiation to support the claims.

data.title: Archer reveals plans for Miami metropolitan air taxi network
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR) on Wednesday unveiled plans for an air taxi network to service the Miami metropolitan area.

The network will see air taxi flights connecting Miami, West Palm Beach, Boca Raton and Fort Lauderdale.

The network will also run connecting air taxi flights between the region’s three major international airports: Miami International Airport, Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood International Airport and Palm Beach International Airport, along with strategic general aviation airports in the area.

Real estate firm Related Ross will develop a vertiport within its West Palm Beach downtown development. Hard Rock Stadium and Apogee Golf Club will ready their existing helipads for electric operations, enabling air taxis.

Dragon Global’s Magic City Innovation District in Miami’s Little Haiti will develop a vertiport site.

Archer’s (ACHR) air taxi network will run its Midnight piloted aircraft, designed to carry four passengers.

Source: Press Release
